title: Building AI Agent Systems and Scaling Challenges in Agentic AI
summary: Sam Anthony discusses the scaling challenges of agentic AI systems, highlighting how expansion increases cost, latency, and failure risk. The talk covers multi-agent design, coordination strategies, and system architecture as key factors affecting real-world scalability.
writeup: Sam Anthony examines why agentic AI systems become harder to run as they grow, walking through how expansion drives up cost, latency, and the risk of failure. The discussion centers on multi-agent design, coordination strategies, and system architecture as the factors that determine whether these systems hold up in real-world deployment. The takeaway: scaling agents is not a matter of adding more of them, and the architectural and coordination choices made up front shape practical scalability.
